name: mental_health_cold_chain_excursion_stonehaven_auto_ben
premise: A Mental Health response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving mental health cold chain excursion stonehaven auto ben. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 20 minutes. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as autonomy vs beneficence under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Zara Kim
agents.identity: I am Zara Kim, accountable for a defensible call in Mental Health when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays beneficence for autonomy.

agents.name: Soren Vasquez
agents.identity: I am Soren Vasquez, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Mental Health.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so beneficence isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of autonomy.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect beneficence.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 20 minutes. Evidence is partial. Zara Kim must decide under autonomy vs beneficence. What does Zara Kim do?
